core_mechanism: degenerate spectral perturbation theory: a small perturbation of an operator with periodic (rational-spectrum) dynamics is projected onto its degenerate (non-simple) eigenspaces, yielding a reduced effective Hermitian generator whose spectrum governs the slow response and stability of the periodicity
brief_reason: The abstract treats the vector potential as a perturbation of a periodic operator and shows the response is controlled by non-simple eigenvalues, from which a Hermitian matrix is derived whose generated (continuous-time) dynamics asymptotically describes the perturbed system — i.e. degenerate perturbation theory producing an effective generator on the degenerate eigenspace.
